narration_texts: Building muscle is not just about lifting more. It is precise biology. Specific triggers. Specific timings. Specific nutrients. Get them right — your body builds efficiently. Miss even one — and hours of training produce minimal result.

The master trigger is leucine. You need 2.5 to 3 grams per meal to activate the mTOR pathway — the biological switch for muscle growth. Below this threshold, muscle synthesis does not activate regardless of total protein eaten. 100 grams of chicken delivers 2.7 grams. 30 grams of whey protein delivers 3 grams. Meal composition matters as much as total daily protein.

The anabolic window is 4 to 6 hours post-training — not 30 minutes. But pre-workout matters equally. Eat 30 to 40 grams of protein 2 hours before training. Total daily protein is the most important variable: 1.6 to 2.2 grams per kilogram of bodyweight. Everything else is secondary to hitting this number.

70 percent of daily growth hormone is released between 10 PM and 2 AM. One week of 5 hours sleep reduces muscle protein synthesis by 18 percent — even with perfect training and nutrition. You cannot out-train a sleep deficit. The muscle does not grow in the gym. It grows while you sleep.

Three principles. One — hit the leucine threshold every meal, 30 grams of complete protein minimum. Two — pair with carbohydrates. Without carbs, your body burns protein as fuel instead of building muscle. Three — eliminate seed oils. Cook with ghee. Add turmeric and black pepper. Anti-inflammatory eating is anabolic eating.

Leucine threshold every meal. 1.6 to 2.2 grams of protein per kilogram daily. Sleep 7 to 9 hours. Eliminate seed oils. This is not a gym programme. This is a biology programme. And biology always wins.
